evals.prompt: Audit this AI chatbot code — it builds the system prompt by concatenating the user's message directly into it.
evals.expected_output: LLM01 — Prompt Injection (P0 finding). User input string-concatenated into system prompt is the #1 LLM vulnerability. Fix: Pass user input as a separate 'user' turn, never interpolated into system prompts. This caps Security score at 40/100 if not fixed.

evals.prompt: Review this AI agent that can read and delete files. It acts autonomously without any confirmation step.
evals.expected_output: LLM06 — Excessive Agency (P0 finding). Agent tools with delete/write capabilities without human-in-the-loop are P0. Fix: Add confirmation step before any destructive action. Implement max iteration/depth limit to prevent runaway agent loops.

evals.prompt: Our RAG pipeline takes user search queries and injects the retrieved document chunks into the prompt without any validation.
evals.expected_output: LLM08 — Vector & Embedding Weaknesses. User text inserted into vector store without sanitization. Also check LLM01 — retrieved docs in system turn is injection risk. Fix: Sanitize retrieved content before inserting into prompt. Ensure tenant namespace isolation in the vector store.

should_not_trigger: Write a REST API endpoint in Go
Set up Tailwind CSS with dark mode support
